description: Periods of active Federal Reserve balance sheet expansion (LSAP / QE), anchored to when markets actually responded — not the FOMC announcement date. The QE detector measures equity momentum + bond stability + M2 growth + equity-bond decorrelation. These signals cannot fire while the market is in crisis (even if the Fed is expanding the balance sheet), so QE1's window starts at the SPY trough (Mar 9 2009) rather than the FOMC announcement (Nov 25 2008). FOMC press releases confirm the program dates; FRED WALCL confirms balance sheet expansion. Operation Twist (Sep 2011 - Dec 2012) is excluded — balance-sheet neutral. Reinvestment periods (balance sheet held flat) are excluded.
dateSource: Federal Reserve FOMC press releases. https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/bst_openmarketops.htm. FRED series WALCL (Fed total assets) for confirmation of expansion periods.

eventWindows.label: QE1 market response: Jun 1 2009 is when the full 63-day momentum lookback is post-trough (trough was Mar 9 2009 + 63 trading days ≈ Jun 4 2009), making a meaningful positive equity momentum reading possible. FOMC announced QE1 Nov 25 2008; purchases concluded Mar 31 2010. Balance sheet $0.9T → $2.3T.
QE2: Announced Nov 3 2010 FOMC. $600B Treasuries at $75B/month. Purchases concluded Jun 30 2011.
QE3: Announced Sep 13 2012 FOMC. Open-ended ($85B/month). Taper began Dec 2013. Final purchase Oct 29 2014.
Post-COVID QE: Unlimited LSAP announced Mar 23 2020 (emergency). Balance sheet peaked ~$9T Jun 2022. First rate hike (and effective end of expansion posture) Mar 16 2022.

passCriterion: Detector fires within 90 trading days of window start. No coverage requirement — the QE signal builds gradually and the equity momentum / decorrelation signals take time to manifest after the announcement.
